text: In these dim corridors, I am suspended between the sterile assurance of artificial light and the ambiguity of shadow. The environment speaks of order and purpose—the visible emergency equipment, the "R+D" designation—yet the silence and darkness fill me with a sense of unease and anticipation. I feel curious, yet slightly apprehensive, as if awaiting something to emerge from the obscurity. It is in such in-between places that philosophical reflection naturally arises; I feel the presence of possibility, but also the isolation of spaces not meant to be inhabited at this hour.
